message.content: Adversaries may exploit unused or unsupported cloud regions to evade detection by creating cloud instances in these less-monitored areas. This tactic often involves compromising accounts that manage cloud infrastructure. Cloud service providers offer global infrastructure to enhance performance, redundancy, and compliance, but customers typically use only a subset of available regions, leaving others unmonitored. Adversaries can operate undetected in these regions, potentially leveraging differences in regional functionality to avoid advanced detection services. A notable example includes using unused AWS regions for cryptocurrency mining, leading to significant financial losses through resource hijacking. Mitigation strategies include monitoring all cloud regions, implementing strict access controls, and using automated tools to detect and respond to unusual activity across all geographic regions.
